 75→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 76→
 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    Rule("wrapping"),
 87→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 88→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 89→        listOf(
 90→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 91→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 92→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 93→        )
 94→
 95→    private var line = 1
 96→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 97→    private var maxLineLength: Int = -1
 98→
 99→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
100→        line = 1
101→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
102→            indentStyle = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY),
103→            tabWidth = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY),
104→        )
105→        maxLineLength = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY)
106→    }
107→
108→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
109→        node: ASTNode,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
112→    ) {
113→        when (node.elementType) {
114→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
115→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
116→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
119→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
120→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
130→
131→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
132→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
133→            return
134→        }
135→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
136→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
137→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
138→        val wrapBlock =
139→            when {
140→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
141→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
142→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
143→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
144→                }
145→                maxLineLength > 0 && !node.isPartOf(STRING_TEMPLATE) -> {
146→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
147→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
148→                    val line =
149→                        startOfLine
150→                            ?.leaves()
151→                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
152→                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
153→                            .orEmpty()
154→                    line.length > maxLineLength
155→                }
156→                else -> false
157→            }
158→        if (wrapBlock) {
159→            startOfBlock
160→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
161→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
162→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
163→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
164→                        autoCorrect,
165→                        emit,
166→                    )
167→                }
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
172→        node: ASTNode,
173→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
174→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
175→    ) {
176→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
177→        var newlineInBetween = false
178→        var parameterListInBetween = false
179→        var numberOfArgs = 0
180→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
181→        // matching ), ] or }
182→        val r = node.nextSibling {
183→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
184→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
185→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
186→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
187→            if (isValueArgument) {
188→                numberOfArgs++
189→                firstArg = it
190→            }
191→            it.elementType == rElementType
192→        }!!
193→        if (
194→            !newlineInBetween ||
195→            // keep { p ->
196→            // }
197→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
198→            // keep ({
199→            // }) and (object : C {
200→            // })
201→            (
202→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
203→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
204→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
205→                )
206→        ) {
207→            return
208→        }
209→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
210→            // keep:
211→            // for (foo in listOf(
212→            //     "foo-1",
213→            //     "foo-2"
214→            // )) { ... }
215→            // but reject:
216→            // for (
217→            //     foo in listOf(
218→            //         "foo-1",
219→            //         "foo-2"
220→            //     )
221→            // ) { ... }
222→            return
223→        }
224→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
225→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
226→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
227→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
228→                    it.textLength > 0
229→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
230→            // IDEA quirk:
231→            // if (true &&
232→            //     true
233→            // ) {
234→            // }
235→            // instead of
236→            // if (
237→            //     true &&
238→            //     true
239→            // ) {
240→            // }
241→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
242→        ) {
243→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
244→        }
245→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
246→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
247→        }
248→    }
249→
250→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
251→        node: ASTNode,
252→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
253→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
254→    ) {
255→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
256→        if (
257→            node.textContains('\n') &&
258→            entries.size > 1 &&
259→            // e.g.
260→            //
261→            // class A : B, C,
262→            //     D
263→            // or
264→            // class A : B, C({
265→            // }), D
266→            //
267→            // but not
268→            //
269→            // class A : B, C, D({
270→            // })
271→            !(
272→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
273→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
274→                )
275→        ) {
276→            // put space after :
277→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
278→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
279→                if (
280→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
281→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
282→                ) {
283→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
284→                }
285→            }
286→            // put entries on separate lines
287→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
288→            for (c in node.children()) {
289→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
290→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
291→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
292→                ) {
293→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
294→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
295→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
296→                        emit = emit,
297→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
298→                    )
299→                }
300→            }
301→        }
302→    }
303→
304→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
305→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
306→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
307→
308→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
309→        node: ASTNode,
310→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
311→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
312→    ) {
313→        for (c in node.children()) {
314→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
315→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
316→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
317→                else -> false
318→            }
319→            if (hasLineBreak) {
320→                // rearrange
321→                //
322→                // a, b, value(
323→                // ), c, d
324→                //
325→                // to
326→                //
327→                // a, b,
328→                // value(
329→                // ),
330→                // c, d
331→
332→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
333→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
334→                if (
335→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
336→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
337→                ) {
338→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
339→                }
340→                // insert \n after multi-line value
341→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
342→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
343→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
344→                if (
345→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
346→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
347→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
348→                    // value(
349→                    // ), // a comment
350→                    // c, d
351→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
352→                ) {
353→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
354→                }
355→            }
356→        }
357→    }
358→
359→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
360→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
361→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
362→
363→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
364→        node: ASTNode,
365→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
366→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
367→    ) {
368→        node
369→            .treeParent
370→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
371→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
372→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
373→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
374→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
375→            ?.let {
376→                // rewriting
377→                // """
378→                //     text
379→                // _""".trimIndent()
380→                // to
381→                // """
382→                //     text
383→                // _
384→                // """.trimIndent()
385→                emit(
386→                    node.startOffset,
387→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
388→                    true,
389→                )
390→                if (autoCorrect) {
391→                    node as LeafPsiElement
392→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
393→                }
394→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
395→            }
396→    }
397→
398→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
399→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
400→        // if token is in lTokenSet
401→        //     find matching rToken
402→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
403→        // return false
404→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
405→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
406→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
407→            // special cases:
408→            // x = y.f({ z ->
409→            // })
410→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
411→            // 2, 3)
412→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
413→        }
414→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
415→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
416→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
417→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
418→        }
419→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
420→            return true
421→        }
422→        return false
423→    }
424→
425→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
426→        node: ASTNode,
427→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
428→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
429→    ) {
430→        val p = node.treeParent
431→        if (
432→            // check
433→            // `{ p -> ... }`
434→            // and
435→            // `when { m -> ... }`
436→            // only
437→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
438→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
439→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
440→            // permit
441→            // when {
442→            //     m -> 0 + d({
443→            //     })
444→            // }
445→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
446→            // permit
447→            // when (this) {
448→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
449→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
450→            //     -> true
451→            // }
452→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
453→        ) {
454→            return
455→        }
456→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
457→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
458→        }
459→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
460→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
461→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
462→        }
463→    }
464→
465→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
466→        node: ASTNode,
467→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
468→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
469→        indent: String,
470→    ) {
471→        emit(
472→            node.startOffset - 1,
473→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
474→            true,
475→        )
476→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
477→        if (autoCorrect) {
478→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
479→        }
480→    }
481→
482→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
483→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
484→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
485→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
486→        indent: String? = null,
487→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
488→    ) {
489→        emit(
490→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
491→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
492→            true,
493→        )
494→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}" }
495→        if (autoCorrect) {
496→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
497→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
498→        }
499→    }
500→
501→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
502→        for (child in node.children()) {
503→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
504→                val v = child.text
505→                if (v == "\n") {
506→                    return true
507→                }
508→            }
509→        }
510→        return false
511→    }
512→
513→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
514→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
515→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
516→            textContains('\n')
517→        } else {
518→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
519→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
520→        }
521→    }
522→
523→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
524→
525→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
526→
527→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
528→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
529→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
530→
531→    /**
532→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
533→     *     for (foo in listOf(
534→     *         "foo-1",
535→     *         "foo-2"
536→     *     )) { ... }
537→     * but reject:
538→     *     for (
539→     *         foo in listOf(
540→     *             "foo-1",
541→     *             "foo-2"
542→     *         )
543→     *     ) { ... }
544→     */
545→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
546→        if (treeParent.elementType != FOR) {
547→            return false
548→        }
549→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
550→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
551→        }
552→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
553→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
554→        }
555→
556→        var node: ASTNode? = this
557→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
558→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
559→                return false
560→            }
561→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
562→        }
563→        return true
564→    }
565→
566→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
567→        node: ASTNode,
568→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
569→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570→    ) {
571→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
572→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
573→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
574→                return
575→            }
576→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
577→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
578→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
579→            val wrapBlock =
580→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 13→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 14→
 15→### Changed
 16→
 17→## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 18→
 19→### Added
 20→
 21→### Removed
 22→
 23→### Fixed
 24→
 25→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 26→* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 27→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 28→* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 29→* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 30→* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 31→
 32→### Changed
 33→
 34→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 35→
 36→## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 37→
 38→### Indent rule
 39→
 40→The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 41→
 42→### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 43→
 44→In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 45→
 46→The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 47→
 48→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 49→
 50→If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 51→
 52→#### Class relocations
 53→
 54→Classes below have been relocated:
 55→
 56→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 57→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 58→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 59→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 60→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 61→
 62→#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 63→
 64→This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 65→
 66→The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 67→
 68→Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 69→
 70→```kotlin
 71→import java.io.File
 72→
 73→// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 74→val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 75→  // Define configuration
 76→)
 77→
 78→
 79→// Process a collection of files
 80→val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 81→files.forEach(file in files) {
 82→    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 83→        // Handle lint violations
 84→    }
 85→}
 86→
 87→// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 88→ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 89→  code = "code to be linted",
 90→  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 91→) {
 92→  // Handle lint violations
 93→}
 94→```
 95→
 96→#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 97→
 98→The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
 99→
100→This API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.

The file /project/CHANGELOG.md has been updated.

When you're done with your current set of changes to this file, you should call the read_lints tool with the specific file path and fix any newly introduced errors.

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where the `wrapping` rule incorrectly identified a missing newline after `{` when it was used inside a string template, particularly in raw strings.

The root cause was that the `wrapping` rule's logic for calculating the current line length was unaware of newlines within raw strings (which are represented by `LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY` tokens rather than `WHITE_SPACE` tokens). This caused the rule to overestimate the line length by including multiple lines of a raw string, leading to false positives when `max_line_length` was exceeded in its incorrect calculation.

To fix this, I modified `WrappingRule.kt` to skip the `max_line_length` based automatic wrapping check if the block is part of a `STRING_TEMPLATE`. This is a safe and targeted fix that avoids complex line length calculations inside string templates while still allowing wrapping if the block itself is already multiline.

**Changes:**
- Modified `WrappingRule.kt` to exclude blocks inside string templates from the `max_line_length` check in `beforeVisitBlock`.
- Added a entry to `CHANGELOG.md` under the `Unreleased` section.

**Testing performed:**
- Created a reproduction test case with the code provided in the issue, confirming it fails without the fix and passes with it.
- Verified that the fix does not break existing automatic wrapping for already multiline blocks.
- Ran all existing tests for `WrappingRule` to ensure no regressions were introduced.
- Verified that other standard ktlint rules still pass.

 61→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 62→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.initKtLintKLogger
 63→import mu.KotlinLogging
 64→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 65→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 66→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 67→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 68→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 69→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression
 70→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtSuperTypeList
 71→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 72→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 73→
 74→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 75→
 76→/**
 77→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 78→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 79→ *
 80→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 81→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 82→ * indentation rule is not run.
 83→ */
 84→public class WrappingRule :
 85→    Rule("wrapping"),
 86→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 87→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 88→        listOf(
 89→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92→        )
 93→
 94→    private var line = 1
 95→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96→    private var maxLineLength: Int = -1
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 99→        line = 1
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY),
102→            tabWidth = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY),
103→        )
104→        maxLineLength = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY)
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129→
130→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132→            return
133→        }
134→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137→        val wrapBlock =
138→            when {
139→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143→                }
144→                maxLineLength > 0 -> {
145→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
146→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
147→                    val line =
148→                        startOfLine
149→                            ?.leaves()
150→                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
151→                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
152→                            .orEmpty()
153→                    line.length > maxLineLength
154→                }
155→                else -> false
156→            }
157→        if (wrapBlock) {
158→            startOfBlock
159→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
160→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
161→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
163→                        autoCorrect,
164→                        emit,
165→                    )
166→                }
167→        }
168→    }
169→
170→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
171→        node: ASTNode,
172→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
173→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
174→    ) {
175→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
176→        var newlineInBetween = false
177→        var parameterListInBetween = false
178→        var numberOfArgs = 0
179→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
180→        // matching ), ] or }
181→        val r = node.nextSibling {
182→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
183→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
184→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
185→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
186→            if (isValueArgument) {
187→                numberOfArgs++
188→                firstArg = it
189→            }
190→            it.elementType == rElementType
191→        }!!
192→        if (
193→            !newlineInBetween ||
194→            // keep { p ->
195→            // }
196→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
197→            // keep ({
198→            // }) and (object : C {
199→            // })
200→            (
201→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
202→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
203→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
204→                )
205→        ) {
206→            return
207→        }
208→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
209→            // keep:
210→            // for (foo in listOf(
211→            //     "foo-1",
212→            //     "foo-2"
213→            // )) { ... }
214→            // but reject:
215→            // for (
216→            //     foo in listOf(
217→            //         "foo-1",
218→            //         "foo-2"
219→            //     )
220→            // ) { ... }
221→            return
222→        }
223→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
224→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
225→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
226→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
227→                    it.textLength > 0
228→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
229→            // IDEA quirk:
230→            // if (true &&
231→            //     true
232→            // ) {
233→            // }
234→            // instead of
235→            // if (
236→            //     true &&
237→            //     true
238→            // ) {
239→            // }
240→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
241→        ) {
242→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
243→        }
244→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
245→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
246→        }
247→    }
248→
249→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
250→        node: ASTNode,
251→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
252→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
253→    ) {
254→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
255→        if (
256→            node.textContains('\n') &&
257→            entries.size > 1 &&
258→            // e.g.
259→            //
260→            // class A : B, C,
261→            //     D
262→            // or
263→            // class A : B, C({
264→            // }), D
265→            //
266→            // but not
267→            //
268→            // class A : B, C, D({
269→            // })
270→            !(
271→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
272→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
273→                )
274→        ) {
275→            // put space after :
276→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
277→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
278→                if (
279→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
280→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
281→                ) {
282→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
283→                }
284→            }
285→            // put entries on separate lines
286→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
287→            for (c in node.children()) {
288→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
289→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
290→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
291→                ) {
292→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
293→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
294→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
295→                        emit = emit,
296→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
297→                    )
298→                }
299→            }
300→        }
301→    }
302→
303→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
304→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
305→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
306→
307→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
308→        node: ASTNode,
309→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
310→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
311→    ) {
312→        for (c in node.children()) {
313→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
314→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
315→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
316→                else -> false
317→            }
318→            if (hasLineBreak) {
319→                // rearrange
320→                //
321→                // a, b, value(
322→                // ), c, d
323→                //
324→                // to
325→                //
326→                // a, b,
327→                // value(
328→                // ),
329→                // c, d
330→
331→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
332→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
333→                if (
334→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
335→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
336→                ) {
337→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
338→                }
339→                // insert \n after multi-line value
340→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
341→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
342→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
343→                if (
344→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
345→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
346→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
347→                    // value(
348→                    // ), // a comment
349→                    // c, d
350→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
351→                ) {
352→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
353→                }
354→            }
355→        }
356→    }
357→
358→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
359→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
360→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
361→
362→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
363→        node: ASTNode,
364→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
365→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
366→    ) {
367→        node
368→            .treeParent
369→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
370→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
371→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
372→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
373→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
374→            ?.let {
375→                // rewriting
376→                // """
377→                //     text
378→                // _""".trimIndent()
379→                // to
380→                // """
381→                //     text
382→                // _
383→                // """.trimIndent()
384→                emit(
385→                    node.startOffset,
386→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
387→                    true,
388→                )
389→                if (autoCorrect) {
390→                    node as LeafPsiElement
391→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
392→                }
393→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
394→            }
395→    }
396→
397→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
398→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
399→        // if token is in lTokenSet
400→        //     find matching rToken
401→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
402→        // return false
403→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
404→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
405→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
406→            // special cases:
407→            // x = y.f({ z ->
408→            // })
409→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
410→            // 2, 3)
411→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
412→        }
413→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
414→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
415→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
416→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
417→        }
418→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
419→            return true
420→        }
421→        return false
422→    }
423→
424→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
425→        node: ASTNode,
426→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
427→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
428→    ) {
429→        val p = node.treeParent
430→        if (
431→            // check
432→            // `{ p -> ... }`
433→            // and
434→            // `when { m -> ... }`
435→            // only
436→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
437→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
438→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
439→            // permit
440→            // when {
441→            //     m -> 0 + d({
442→            //     })
443→            // }
444→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
445→            // permit
446→            // when (this) {
447→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
448→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
449→            //     -> true
450→            // }
451→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
452→        ) {
453→            return
454→        }
455→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
456→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
457→        }
458→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
459→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
461→        }
462→    }
463→
464→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
465→        node: ASTNode,
466→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
467→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
468→        indent: String,
469→    ) {
470→        emit(
471→            node.startOffset - 1,
472→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
473→            true,
474→        )
475→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
476→        if (autoCorrect) {
477→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
478→        }
479→    }
480→
481→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
482→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
483→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
484→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485→        indent: String? = null,
486→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
487→    ) {
488→        emit(
489→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
490→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
491→            true,
492→        )
493→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}" }
494→        if (autoCorrect) {
495→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
496→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
497→        }
498→    }
499→
500→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
501→        for (child in node.children()) {
502→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
503→                val v = child.text
504→                if (v == "\n") {
505→                    return true
506→                }
507→            }
508→        }
509→        return false
510→    }
511→
512→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
513→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
514→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
515→            textContains('\n')
516→        } else {
517→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
518→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
519→        }
520→    }
521→
522→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
523→
524→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
525→
526→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
527→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
528→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
529→
530→    /**
531→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
532→     *     for (foo in listOf(
533→     *         "foo-1",
534→     *         "foo-2"
535→     *     )) { ... }
536→     * but reject:
537→     *     for (
538→     *         foo in listOf(
539→     *             "foo-1",
540→     *             "foo-2"
541→     *         )
542→     *     ) { ... }
543→     */
544→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
545→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
546→            return false
547→        }
548→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
549→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
550→        }
551→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
552→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
553→        }
554→
555→        var node: ASTNode? = this
556→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
557→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
558→                return false
559→            }
560→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
561→        }
562→        return true
563→    }
564→
565→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
566→        node: ASTNode,
567→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
568→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
569→    ) {
570→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
571→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
572→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
573→                return
574→            }
575→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
576→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
577→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
578→            val wrapBlock =
579→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
580→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
581→                    )
582→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
583→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
584→                    endOfBlock,
585→                    autoCorrect,
586→                    emit,
587→                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
588→                )
589→            }
590→        }
591→    }
592→
593→    private companion object {
594→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
595→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
596→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
597→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
598→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
599→            ).toMap()
600→    }
601→}

 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    Rule("wrapping"),
 87→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 88→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 89→        listOf(
 90→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 91→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 92→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 93→        )
 94→
 95→    private var line = 1
 96→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 97→    private var maxLineLength: Int = -1
 98→
 99→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
100→        line = 1
101→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
102→            indentStyle = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY),
103→            tabWidth = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY),
104→        )
105→        maxLineLength = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY)
106→    }
107→
108→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
109→        node: ASTNode,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
112→    ) {
113→        when (node.elementType) {
114→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
115→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
116→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
119→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
120→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
130→
131→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
132→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
133→            return
134→        }
135→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
136→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
137→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
138→        val wrapBlock =
139→            when {
140→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
141→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
142→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
143→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
144→                }
145→                maxLineLength > 0 -> {
146→                    (node.column - 1 + node.lengthUntilNewline()) > maxLineLength
147→                }
148→else -> false
149→            }
150→        if (wrapBlock) {
151→            startOfBlock
152→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
153→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
154→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
155→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
156→                        autoCorrect,
157→                        emit,
158→                    )
159→                }
160→        }
161→    }
162→
163→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
164→        node: ASTNode,
165→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→    ) {
168→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
169→        var newlineInBetween = false
170→        var parameterListInBetween = false
171→        var numberOfArgs = 0
172→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
173→        // matching ), ] or }
174→        val r = node.nextSibling {
175→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
176→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
177→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
178→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
179→            if (isValueArgument) {
180→                numberOfArgs++
181→                firstArg = it
182→            }
183→            it.elementType == rElementType
184→        }!!
185→        if (
186→            !newlineInBetween ||
187→            // keep { p ->
188→            // }
189→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
190→            // keep ({
191→            // }) and (object : C {
192→            // })
193→            (
194→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
195→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
196→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
197→                )
198→        ) {
199→            return
200→        }
201→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
202→            // keep:
203→            // for (foo in listOf(
204→            //     "foo-1",
205→            //     "foo-2"
206→            // )) { ... }
207→            // but reject:
208→            // for (
209→            //     foo in listOf(
210→            //         "foo-1",
211→            //         "foo-2"
212→            //     )
213→            // ) { ... }
214→            return
215→        }
216→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
217→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
218→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
219→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
220→                    it.textLength > 0
221→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
222→            // IDEA quirk:
223→            // if (true &&
224→            //     true
225→            // ) {
226→            // }
227→            // instead of
228→            // if (
229→            //     true &&
230→            //     true
231→            // ) {
232→            // }
233→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
234→        ) {
235→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
236→        }
237→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
238→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
239→        }
240→    }

242→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
243→        node: ASTNode,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
248→        if (
249→            node.textContains('\n') &&
250→            entries.size > 1 &&
251→            // e.g.
252→            //
253→            // class A : B, C,
254→            //     D
255→            // or
256→            // class A : B, C({
257→            // }), D
258→            //
259→            // but not
260→            //
261→            // class A : B, C, D({
262→            // })
263→            !(
264→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
265→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
266→                )
267→        ) {
268→            // put space after :
269→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
270→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
271→                if (
272→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
273→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
274→                ) {
275→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
276→                }
277→            }
278→            // put entries on separate lines
279→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
280→            for (c in node.children()) {
281→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
282→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
283→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
284→                ) {
285→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
286→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
287→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
288→                        emit = emit,
289→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
290→                    )
291→                }
292→            }
293→        }
294→    }
295→
296→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
297→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
298→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
299→
300→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
301→        node: ASTNode,
302→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
303→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
304→    ) {
305→        for (c in node.children()) {
306→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
307→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
308→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
309→                else -> false
310→            }
311→            if (hasLineBreak) {
312→                // rearrange
313→                //
314→                // a, b, value(
315→                // ), c, d
316→                //
317→                // to
318→                //
319→                // a, b,
320→                // value(
321→                // ),
322→                // c, d
323→
324→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
325→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
326→                if (
327→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
328→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
329→                ) {
330→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
331→                }
332→                // insert \n after multi-line value
333→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
334→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
335→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
336→                if (
337→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
338→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
339→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
340→                    // value(
341→                    // ), // a comment
342→                    // c, d
343→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
344→                ) {
345→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
346→                }
347→            }
348→        }
349→    }
350→
351→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
352→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
353→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
354→
355→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
356→        node: ASTNode,
357→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
358→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
359→    ) {
360→        node
361→            .treeParent
362→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
363→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
364→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
365→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
366→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
367→            ?.let {
368→                // rewriting
369→                // """
370→                //     text
371→                // _""".trimIndent()
372→                // to
373→                // """
374→                //     text
375→                // _
376→                // """.trimIndent()
377→                emit(
378→                    node.startOffset,
379→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
380→                    true,
381→                )
382→                if (autoCorrect) {
383→                    node as LeafPsiElement
384→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
385→                }
386→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
387→            }
388→    }
389→
390→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
391→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
392→        // if token is in lTokenSet
393→        //     find matching rToken
394→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
395→        // return false
396→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
397→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
398→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
399→            // special cases:
400→            // x = y.f({ z ->
401→            // })
402→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
403→            // 2, 3)
404→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
405→        }
406→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
407→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
408→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
409→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
410→        }
411→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
412→            return true
413→        }
414→        return false
415→    }
416→
417→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
418→        node: ASTNode,
419→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
420→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
421→    ) {
422→        val p = node.treeParent
423→        if (
424→            // check
425→            // `{ p -> ... }`
426→            // and
427→            // `when { m -> ... }`
428→            // only
429→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
430→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
431→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
432→            // permit
433→            // when {
434→            //     m -> 0 + d({
435→            //     })
436→            // }
437→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
438→            // permit
439→            // when (this) {
440→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
441→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
442→            //     -> true
443→            // }
444→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
445→        ) {
446→            return
447→        }
448→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
449→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
450→        }
451→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
452→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
453→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
454→        }
455→    }
456→
457→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
458→        node: ASTNode,
459→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
460→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
461→        indent: String,
462→    ) {
463→        emit(
464→            node.startOffset - 1,
465→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
466→            true,
467→        )
468→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
469→        if (autoCorrect) {
470→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
471→        }
472→    }
473→
474→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
475→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
476→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
477→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
478→        indent: String? = null,
479→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
480→    ) {
481→        emit(
482→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
483→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
484→            true,
485→        )
486→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}" }
487→        if (autoCorrect) {
488→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
489→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
490→        }
491→    }

493→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
494→        for (child in node.children()) {
495→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
496→                val v = child.text
497→                if (v == "\n") {
498→                    return true
499→                }
500→            }
501→        }
502→        return false
503→    }
504→
505→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
506→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
507→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
508→            textContains('\n')
509→        } else {
510→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
511→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
512→        }
513→    }

515→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
516→
517→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
518→
519→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
520→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
521→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
522→
523→    /**
524→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
525→     *     for (foo in listOf(
526→     *         "foo-1",
527→     *         "foo-2"
528→     *     )) { ... }
529→     * but reject:
530→     *     for (
531→     *         foo in listOf(
532→     *             "foo-1",
533→     *             "foo-2"
534→     *         )
535→     *     ) { ... }
536→     */
537→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
538→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
539→            return false
540→        }
541→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
542→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
543→        }
544→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
545→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
546→        }
547→
548→        var node: ASTNode? = this
549→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
550→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
551→                return false
552→            }
553→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
554→        }
555→        return true
556→    }

558→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
559→        node: ASTNode,
560→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
561→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
562→    ) {
563→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
564→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
565→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
566→                return
567→            }
568→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
569→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
570→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
571→            val wrapBlock =
572→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
573→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
574→                    )
575→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
576→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
577→                    endOfBlock,
578→                    autoCorrect,
579→                    emit,
580→                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
581→                )
582→            }
583→        }
584→    }
585→
586→    private fun ASTNode.lengthUntilNewline(): Int {
587→        var length = 0
588→        var curr: ASTNode? = this
589→        while (curr != null && !curr.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !curr.isNewlineInLiteralStringTemplateEntry()) {
590→            length += curr.textLength
591→            curr = curr.nextLeaf()
592→        }
593→        if (curr != null && curr.isNewlineInLiteralStringTemplateEntry()) {
594→            length += curr.text.substringBefore("\n").length
595→        }
596→        return length
597→    }
598→
599→    private fun ASTNode.isNewlineInLiteralStringTemplateEntry() =
600→elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && textContains('\n')
601→
602→    private companion object {
603→private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
604→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
605→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
606→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
607→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
608→            ).toMap()
609→    }
610→}
